highlights.text: I wrote last Friday at 4pm: “My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
This morning around 9:30AM, for the first time in a few weeks it felt like, my “bear case” triggered.
I wrote yesterday at 4pm: “Bear case tomorrow/today: Begins under 6982… For the 6982 short ideally one sees a final bounce there and/or Failed Breakdown.

As always, we buy dips the exact same way institutions do: Institutions buy dips by waiting for a news driven elevator down flush/trap , waiting for a low to be taken out in the process, trapping shorts beneath the low, using that liquidity to fund their longs, and then price rips when the low recovers We saw this dynamic nearly everyday the last week and it started right off Sunday’s open.
Today, ES was tested with another deep elevator down flush/trap.
I wrote yesterday at 3pm: “There is some support at 6923 but instead of buying this wait for the Failed Breakdown of 6923.

We set a huge low Monday morning at 4:45AM at 6923 and the flush and recovery of it is actionable.” By 11:20AM, we bounced at ~6900, recovered 6923, the Failed Breakdown was triggered, and we ripped to 6970+.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow.

post_title: Deep Flush For SPX. Will Bulls Buy This Dip? Jan 30th Plan
